axes.gap: Four-axis client can neither SEND tools nor RECEIVE tool_calls. (1) CompletionRequest carries no tools/tool_choice and extra='forbid' hard-rejects them; (2) every chat wire adapter's build_request_payload omits the provider-specific tool block, and openai_chat.parse_response discards tool_calls — no normalized cross-wire tool_call shape exists; (3) nothing surfaces tool_calls back to a caller, so a multi-round execute-append-recall loop cannot be built on complete(). The loop itself is agent-layer (LLM-first) but structurally cannot exist until complete() returns tool_calls.

axes.gap: Four-axis `stream()` has no delta-accumulation layer: it yields raw per-line parsed chunks, and the shapers drop tool_call deltas entirely. Three missing pieces: (1) `CompletionRequest` cannot carry `tools`/`tool_choice` so no tool-streaming can be requested (prerequisite, owned by the tool-calling-request axis); (2) `openai_chat.parse_response` (and mistral_chat, the OpenAI-compat sibling) does not surface `choices[0].delta.tool_calls` on the chunk dict, and `anthropic_messages.parse_response` does not surface `content_block_start`(tool_use)/`input_json_delta.partial_json`; (3) `stream()` has no stateful index-keyed accumulator to fold those deltas across chunks into a final aggregated `tool_calls` list — the legacy `tool_call_acc`→`sorted()`→terminal-`done` behavior (openai.py:535/566-586/598). The four-axis stream is byte-preserving text-only today.
axes.legacy_impl: Legacy accumulates OpenAI tool-call deltas inside the provider's own streaming loop. `kaizen/providers/llm/openai.py::OpenAIProvider.stream_chat` (def at openai.py:449) folds partial tool_call deltas keyed by chunk index: `tool_call_acc: dict[int, dict] = {}` (openai.py:535); per-chunk fold `for tc_delta in delta.tool_calls: idx=tc_delta.index; acc=setdefault(idx,{id,type,function{name,arguments}}); acc['id']=tc_delta.id; acc['function']['name']+=name_piece; acc['function']['arguments']+=args_piece` (openai.py:566-586); final assembly `final_tool_calls=[tool_call_acc[k] for k in sorted(tool_call_acc)]` (openai.py:598) emitted on a terminal `StreamEvent(event_type='done', tool_calls=final_tool_calls, ...)` (openai.py:599-606). The event carrier is `StreamEvent` (kaizen/providers/types.py:118-140; `tool_calls: list[dict]` field at types.py:136). NOTE: only the OpenAI provider accumulates tool deltas — the legacy anthropic provider's stream_chat (anthropic.py:282, accumulates only text at anthropic.py:332-376) does NOT accumulate tool_use deltas, so legacy tool-streaming parity is OpenAI-family only. Entry point `get_streaming_provider(name_or_model)` at registry.py:206-225 (returns a StreamingProvider protocol impl; `StreamingProvider` protocol declared base.py:110-129).
axes.fouraxis_today: Four-axis `LlmClient.stream()` (client.py:1014) is a STATELESS per-line passthrough: the SSE loop `async for line in http_client.stream_lines(...): chunk=_parse_stream_line(line, shaper); if chunk is not None: yield chunk` (client.py:1107-1116) yields each parsed chunk independently with ZERO cross-chunk state. `_parse_stream_line` (client.py:183-209) calls `shaper.parse_response(obj)` per line. `openai_chat.parse_response` (openai_chat.py:91-124) extracts `delta.content` text (openai_chat.py:107-113) but completely IGNORES `delta.tool_calls`. `anthropic_messages.parse_response` (anthropic_messages.py:152-183) returns `raw_blocks` for the NON-streaming block list; it has no `content_block_delta`/`input_json_delta`/`partial_json` streaming-tool handling. `CompletionRequest` (deployment.py:242-259) carries model/messages/temperature/top_p/max_tokens/stop/stream/user ONLY — no `tools`/`tool_choice`, so `stream()` cannot even REQUEST tools. Consumer check: `get_streaming_provider` is referenced only by registry.py:206 (self) and tests/unit/providers/test_registry.py — NO production agent path consumes the four-axis stream-with-tools capability yet.
